response.justification: Bogdan Szabo is a strong TypeScript/Node.js full-stack engineer with broad polyglot experience, but his profile is fundamentally misaligned with this role. C++ exposure is minimal — roughly six months at National Instruments in 2014–2015 and a bachelor thesis project — far short of the mandatory 5–10 years of hands-on C++ on Windows. There is no evidence of capital markets, fixed income, or investment banking domain knowledge, no SQL Server or Oracle experience, and no mention of Summit, STL, Python, Kafka, or financial messaging systems. His single fintech touch-point (RIDE Capital) was entirely Node/TypeScript/GraphQL with no C++ or financial-product involvement.
strengths: Solid software craftsmanship practices: TDD, CI/CD, trunk-based development, pairing — all well demonstrated across multiple roles
Exposure to a financial-services context at RIDE Capital (investment platform), providing some domain adjacency
Strong communication and knowledge-sharing track record evidenced by conference talks, workshops, and open-source project leadership
concerns: C++ experience is superficial and dated (~6 months in 2014–2015); nowhere near the mandatory 5–10 years of hands-on C++ on Windows
Zero fixed income, fixed income derivatives, capital markets, or investment banking domain expertise in any technical capacity
No demonstrated SQL Server or Oracle experience; primary database background is MongoDB and MySQL, with SQL listed only as a skill keyword

key_factors.factor: No capital markets / fixed income domain knowledge
Strong engineering practices and communication skills
